body.title: Inference device: structured summary handoff between model tiers
body.result: escalation_hop+prior_attempt on InferenceRequest; dispatch prepends handoff to system; 2-hop ceiling; 6 tests green
body.intention: I intend that when the inference device escalates from a cheaper model to a more expensive one, it passes the cheaper model's attempt summary plus what now? rather than re-sending the original prompt cold.
body.description:
Tiered inference (start cheap, escalate if stuck) is incomplete without a handoff artifact. Currently a cheap model failure sends the original prompt cold to the expensive model — re-deriving what was already tried.

Design and implement: (1) cheap model attempt produces structured summary when it cannot close the task; (2) inference device assembles [original context] + [attempt summary] + What now?; (3) expensive model receives this and picks up informed. Pattern mirrors advisor() but adds the structured summary layer. Hard ceiling: max 2 escalation hops — enforce in device code.

**Affected files:** devices/inference/ (specific files TBD in sprint — discovery step)
**Design rules:** OOP-first (BaseDevice); log every tier transition at INFO with model-from, model-to, summary-length
**Scope boundary:** Inference device internal tier escalation only. External API unchanged. DickSimnel agent escalation is a separate ticket.
**Completion criteria:** Tier escalation logs INFO line with model-from/to/summary-length. Expensive model prompt includes attempt summary section. Max 2 hops enforced. Integration test covers the escalation path.
